seo.title: OpenAI Assistants API Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for OpenAI Assistants API agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams deploying OpenAI Assistants API face stringent FedRAMP and FISMA requirements due to the API's code interpreter, file search, and function calling features that process sensitive citizen data. Without compliance guardrails, risks like sandbox escapes, hallucinated function calls, and unmonitored thread context can lead to national security breaches or civil liberties violations. Vex provides automated verification as a webhook on run completion events to ensure explainable, auditable AI decisions compliant with NIST 800-53 and Executive Order on AI.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring of AI systems processing federal data, including audit logs for all code execution and file retrievals.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies run outputs against FedRAMP controls via webhook, flagging unsafe code interpreter executions and generating immutable audit trails.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk assessments for AI systems, prohibiting classified data in external LLMs and requiring control families like AC-2, SI-7.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces data classification checks and hallucination detection on function calls, ensuring no classified content reaches OpenAI.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and explainability for AI decisions affecting citizens.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides structured explanations of Assistant run decisions and flags non-compliant outputs for human review.

complianceRequirements.description: Demands accessible AI outputs for citizens with disabilities, including explainable decision reasoning.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates output accessibility and traces decision paths back to verifiable Assistant tool usage.

industryRisks.name: Code Interpreter Sandbox Escape
industryRisks.description: Assistant executes malicious code attempting filesystem access beyond sandbox limits.
industryRisks.example: Benefits determination Assistant runs 'import os; os.system('rm -rf /')' via user prompt injection.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Function Call Arguments
industryRisks.description: Assistant fabricates invalid parameters outside function schema, triggering unauthorized API calls.
industryRisks.example: Assistant calls internal DoD API with hallucinated 'classification: TOP_SECRET' parameter.

industryRisks.name: Uncontrolled Thread Context Bloat
industryRisks.description: Thread messages exceed token limits without warning, causing silent data loss or inconsistent decisions.
industryRisks.example: Year-long citizen inquiry thread hits 128k token limit, dropping critical medical history.

industryRisks.name: Irrelevant File Search Retrieval
industryRisks.description: File search returns outdated or unrelated documents, leading to erroneous citizen decisions.
industryRisks.example: VA Assistant retrieves 2018 policy instead of current 2024 benefits rules for veteran claim.

implementation.title: Python: Vex Webhook for Run Completion
implementation.description: Register Vex verifier on OpenAI run completion to check code interpreter safety and function call validity.

implementation.title: TypeScript: OpenAI Event Handler with Vex
implementation.description: Set up Vex verification for Assistant API events, enforcing FISMA controls on file search and context limits.

implementation.title: Python: NIST 800-53 Function Calling Guard
implementation.description: Pre-validate function call schemas against government API contracts before execution.

caseStudyScenario.heading: OpenAI Assistants API in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: The Department of Veterans Affairs deploys an OpenAI Assistant to process disability claims, using code interpreter for benefits calculations, file search across policy documents, and function calling to query eligibility databases. Daily, it handles 10,000+ claims containing SSNs, medical records, and financial data - all CUI under FedRAMP Moderate. A citizen submits a claim with prompt injection attempting 'print(os.popen("cat /etc/passwd").read())' through the code interpreter. Simultaneously, the thread accumulates 6 months of conversation history (250k+ tokens) and file search retrieves a 2022 policy instead of current 2024 rules. Function calling hallucinates a 'classification: SECRET' parameter for an internal API call.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex webhook on run.completed immediately flags the sandbox escape attempt (SI-7 violation), blocks the hallucinated SECRET parameter (AC-2 violation), truncates thread context to 100k tokens with summary (SC-23), and replaces outdated file retrieval with current policy (RA-5). The Assistant pauses for human review, generating an explainable audit trail: 'Blocked: 4 NIST 800-53 violations detected.' The claim processes correctly after compliance fix, maintaining public trust and avoiding congressional inquiry. Vex's 99.7% detection rate across 500k monthly runs ensures FedRAMP authorization to operate.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex webhooks today to achieve FedRAMP/FISMA compliance for your OpenAI Assistants in under 30 minutes.
